programme.title: Mehek
programme.desc: Zindagi Ki Mehek is an Indian television drama series that aired from 2016 to 2018. The story revolves around Mehek Sharma, a talented cook from a middle-class family, and Shaurya Khanna, a wealthy and arrogant restaurateur. Their paths cross when Mehek participates in a cooking competition that Shaurya is judging. Despite their contrasting personalities and initial conflicts, they fall in love and face numerous challenges together, including family opposition and personal vendettas. The show blends themes of love, family, and ambition with a strong emphasis on culinary arts.

programme.title: Dholi Beats
programme.desc: The story revolves around Geet, a young woman who follows her father's footsteps as a dhol player, challenging societal norms about gender roles in music. Her journey is supported by her husband, Malhar, who helps her pursue her dreams.

programme.title: King of Hearts
programme.desc: Jamai Raja is an Indian drama series about Siddharth, who marries Roshni to mend her strained relationship with her mother, Durga Devi Patel. The show explores the complexities of family dynamics, love, and duty as Siddharth navigates various challenges to unite his new family.

programme.title: Second Chances
programme.desc: Yash, a rich and esteemed business tycoon, yearns for real love. While he falls in love with Neha, the journey ahead is not going to be an easy one for him. Will he be able to win Nehas heart?

programme.title: Jodha Akbar
programme.desc: Jodha Akbar is a period drama set in the sixteenth century, focusing on the political marriage between Mughal Emperor Akbar and Rajput Princess Jodha Bai. Initially a marriage of convenience, it evolves into a deep love story that changes the fate of India. The series portrays the era's wars, the complex relationships between the Mughals and Rajputs, and the political dynamics of the royal court. Akbar, raised as a ruthless conqueror, learns the meaning of love and compassion through Jodha, who despises the Mughals for their invasions. Their relationship transforms Akbar from a heartless ruler into the beloved Emperor Akbar, known for his justice and wisdom.

programme.title: The Good Son
programme.desc: Raja Beta is an Indian television drama series that revolves around the life of Vedant Tripathi, an ideal and dutiful son. programme.desc: A look at one of The Open's great champions. Here, the focus is on Nick Price, who won his sole Claret Jug back in 1994 at Turnberry.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccer Box
programme.desc: Gary Neville sits down with former teammate and opponent Dimitar Berbatov to discuss their time in the game and some of the most notable matches in which they played.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccer Box
programme.desc: Gary Neville is joined by one of England's greatest-ever goalscorers, Alan Shearer, to look back on the most memorable matches from his career.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccer Box
programme.desc: Gary Neville sits down with Ian Wright to discuss their time in the game and some of the most notable matches in which they played.

programme.title: Gary Neville's Soccer Box
programme.desc: Gary Neville sits down with former teammate Paul Scholes to discuss their time in the game and some of the most notable matches in which they played.

programme.title: Sporting Heroes
programme.desc: Cricketer Steve Harmison interviews former Liverpool player and Newcastle United manager Kevin Keegan about his prolific career. Keegan was twice named European Player of the Year.

programme.title: Sporting Heroes
programme.desc: Gary Newbon interviews Olympic champion swimmer Rebecca Adlington about her glittering career in the sport. Adlington claimed two gold medals at the 2008 games in Beijing.

programme.title: Off Limits
programme.desc: Ahead of her bout with Alycia Baumgardner, Mikaela Mayer sits down with Anna Woolhouse to discuss the challenges of her life before boxing and her journey towards becoming world champion.

programme.title: Off Limits
programme.desc: Ahead of his rematch with Chris Eubank Jr, Liam Smith sits down with Anna Woolhouse to discuss his family's journey through boxing and life away from the ring.

programme.title: Signs of Evil
programme.desc: The symbol of the SS, Hitler's Schutzstaffel, stands for terror and the murder of millions. The penniless graphic artist Walter Heck was given the job of designing the jagged, rune-like characters in 1929. The “sig” rune was rooted deep in the ideological cosmos of the Nazis and their supporters. They saw themselves as the descendants of the Teutons and declared the runes to be humanity's original Germanic script. The symbol was meant to unite Heinrich Himmler's “Black Order.” But did it escape their notice that it was originally of Semitic origin? In October 1945, the Allies passed a law banning the Nazi Party and all associated organizations, including the SS. The young Federal Republic even forbade all Nazi symbols. This documentary tells the gripping story of the SS symbol, from the Middle Ages to the present day.

programme.title: Monsters and Myths
programme.desc: Monsters have always been part of our nightmares and fairy tales. But where do they actually come from? How much of them is truth, how much is myth? Going to great lengths in this documentary, the filmmakers join scientists to explore a number of the best known sagas and legends in the world.

programme.title: Expedition Wallacea
programme.desc: 150 years ago Alfred Wallace used his observations on Indonesian islands to develop a theory of evolution independently of Darwin's. Now Matthias Kopfmüller's team want to add to Wallace's work: can the undersea evidence explain what Wallace found on land? What Wallace had recognised is that within a single island group there seemed to be a barrier that species could not pass.

programme.title: Urban Jungle
programme.desc: We think cities belong to us, but the concrete jungle's teaming with animals - whether its monkeys in Japan, sparrows in Germany, Turkish rats or even Manatee in Florida. We make friends with some, but most go about their lives unseen. Some people see wild animals as pests, and dangerous to our health, others believe they are crucial to our well-being and belong here as much as we do.

programme.title: Signs of Evil
programme.desc: The symbol of the SS, Hitler's Schutzstaffel, stands for terror and the murder of millions. The penniless graphic artist Walter Heck was given the job of designing the jagged, rune-like characters in 1929. The “sig” rune was rooted deep in the ideological cosmos of the Nazis and their supporters. They saw themselves as the descendants of the Teutons and declared the runes to be humanity's original Germanic script. The symbol was meant to unite Heinrich Himmler's “Black Order.” But did it escape their notice that it was originally of Semitic origin? In October 1945, the Allies passed a law banning the Nazi Party and all associated organizations, including the SS. The young Federal Republic even forbade all Nazi symbols. This documentary tells the gripping story of the SS symbol, from the Middle Ages to the present day.

programme.title: Monsters and Myths
programme.desc: Monsters have always been part of our nightmares and fairy tales. But where do they actually come from? How much of them is truth, how much is myth? Going to great lengths in this documentary, the filmmakers join scientists to explore a number of the best known sagas and legends in the world.

programme.title: The Great Wall of China
programme.desc: Since the earliest civilizations, humans have needed walls to protect them. From rudimentary walls around early towns and cities to vast fortresses and castles, culminating in one of the greatest engineering triumphs of all time – the Great Wall of China.

programme.title: Secrets Of Angkor Wat
programme.desc: Temples, designed to honor the Gods, have been at the heart of cities since earliest times. Often they have been the biggest and most impressive buildings of their age. But mysterious ancient temples crop up across the ancient world. Why are so many of these monuments carefully aligned with the sun?

programme.title: The Power Of Water
programme.desc: Without water, civilization is simply not possible. Romans took control of their water supplies very, very seriously. In order to keep the taps on, Roman engineers constructed vast networks of aqueducts, tunnels, sewers, and drains.

programme.title: The Taj Mahal And The Golden Age Of Islam
programme.desc: Awe-inspiring palaces are the legacy that demonstrates the brilliance of Islamic engineers, working centuries before Western Europe began its own engineering revolution. It's a process that culminated in what is arguably the most beautiful building in the World – the Taj Mahal.

programme.title: History's Greatest Ships
programme.desc: As far back as the Bronze Age, humans were building ships capable of crossing oceans and trading between continents. Perhaps the greatest surviving vessel from the Ancient world, is The Mary Rose, Henry VIII's mighty warship. It was a true engineering wonder, but one built with a fatal weakness.

programme.title: Secrets Of Angkor Wat
programme.desc: Temples, designed to honor the Gods, have been at the heart of cities since earliest times. Often they have been the biggest and most impressive buildings of their age. But mysterious ancient temples crop up across the ancient world. Why are so many of these monuments carefully aligned with the sun?

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: There is cause for celebration in the orang-utang house, while the world's oldest woolly monkey has a very bad day.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The new orang-utan nursery is finally open but, despite a storm, new mum Hsiao-Quai refuses to bring her baby indoors.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Paddy's chimps go mad for mangoes and rumours abound of a weighty new arrival from South Africa. Plus, Alison saves an old pet squirrel monkey that desperately needs a new home.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The team wants to bring a seriously overweight orang-utan to the UK, but it is harder than they hoped, and back at Monkey World the Bachelor boys celebrate a bedding bonanza.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: There's a problem at the airport with the transportation of overweight orang-utan Oshine from South Africa - will she make it onto the plane?

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Oshine has an eventful first night in the orang-utan nursery and there is exciting news for the woolly monkeys. Plus, Hananya's gang go wild over the revamped chimp enclosure.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Mike has a tricky time collecting a baby gibbon from France. Meanwhile, at Monkey World, the chimps have fun with fabric, and squirrel monkey Fidget settles into his new home.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: It's handbags at dawn in the orang-utan nursery as new arrival Oshine meets foster mum A-mei. Piquita becomes a new mum, and Mikado settles in after his first week at the park.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Tensions are high as popular chimp, Sammy, goes under the knife, and the stump-tailed macaque house gets a makeover to accommodate its elderly ugly monkeys.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: It's a big day for baby golden-cheeked gibbon Mikado as he's introduced to his new foster mum Alex, and Gordon's orang-utan group have a bit of fun with some colourful balls.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Staff are anxious as chimp Athena undergoes an eye operation and woolly monkey Piquita's baby is finally given a name.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The staff prepares for two new woolly monkeys from Switzerland, and the male capuchins are put to the test with a clever puzzle feeder.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: It's the stuff of nightmares. Emergency lines flooded with calls as UFOs appear in the sky. Mounting evidence has revealed that UFO waves are able to penetrate the world's most closely guarded airspace.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: The tourist-attracting waters of Crater Lake National Park may also be the epicenter of dark and sinister forces from Native American lore that lure visitors to their deaths.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A demonic truck driver, a time loop, disembodied voices, and nightmarish skinwalkers are among the unexplained phenomena at Canyonlands National Park. Quartz could be acting as a recorder of human experience.

programme.title: What On Earth
programme.desc: Embark on an eye-opening journey as experts investigate the startling mysteries captured by satellites orbiting the Earth: from hidden worlds to unexplainable natural phenomena.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A series of occurrences taking place over several years in Big Bend National Park, in Texas, are seen by many experts to indicate the presence of extraterrestrial activity.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A series of deaths and disappearances in Death Valley National Park leads to the discovery of forces that are believed to inhabit the park's secret underworld.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Conspiracy Files examines a conspiracy that may be seeking to eradicate 90% of the world's population. It looks at the Georgia Guidestones built by an unknown architect which call for humanity to reduce its population to below 500 million.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Conspiracy Files examines whether the United State military has constructed flying saucers. It looks at unclassified documents describing Project 1794 and a supersonic large disk fighter aircraft. These plans are later developed by a Canadian company Avro Aircraft into the Avrocar.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Conspiracy Files looks at a number of end-of-the-world scenarios and considers the inevitability of doomsday. It also looks at the question of whether world governments would conspire with each other to cover up knowledge of the end-of-the-world.

programme.title: Conspiracy Files Unsealed
programme.desc: There is a battle going on for your mind. From the Cold War to the TV set, someone wants to control what you think, how you act and what you believe. We'll reveal the new practices in mind control.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Scientists have never been closer to proving that life once lived on Mars. But is it still there? And if so, how has it evolved?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Scientists and Ufologists are scouring the vast regions of space for signs of extraterrestrial life.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Some scientists believe the survival of mankind hinges upon the revolutionary science of Artificial Intelligence.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: For decades, scientists have been searching for proof that extraterrestrials exist. But just where does alien life exist? And what does it look like?

programme.title: S1 E1 First Apocalypse
programme.desc: Clues to human extinction found in Dinosaur's apocalypse.

programme.title: S1 E1 Black Blizzard
programme.desc: Experience 1930-1940 U.S. history with recreated black blizzards.

programme.desc: Human civilization is under attack from a catastrophic--but unseen--threat.

programme.desc: The most powerful blast of cosmic radiation in the Universe.

programme.title: Wolves
programme.desc: Wolves: icons of the wilderness, spectacular creatures that live and die by the sword.

programme.title: Seasons Of The Otter
programme.desc: Most of the life of an otter goes unseen following a young otter family we'll reveal the secrets of the otter's world.

programme.title: Nuts about Squirrels
programme.desc: Squirrels have conquered tundra, deserts, forests and more, developing ingenious life skills along the way, from stashing nuts to fighting snakes.

programme.title: Mountains
programme.desc: Mountains define some of our most spectacular landscapes, creating a mosaic of dramatic habitats, teaming with incredible wildlife. Mountains are found on every continent on Earth, covering 22% of the planet's surface. They're a vital part of our ecosystem a quarter of terrestrial animal and plant species live in mountains and they house an incredible 85% of all amphibians, mammals and birds. It's no wonder that so many cultures consider mountains sacred places: gateways to the heavens.

programme.title: Fight for Territory
programme.desc: Biological instinct is driven by three things: the desire to stay alive, to find food and to breed. But to do all these things, animals first need a home. Out in the wilderness a safe place to call home can be the difference between life and death. A place to secure meals, raise their families and hide from danger. Ideal spots are in short supply and well worth fighting for! From lion scraps to insect warfare, these conflicts can provide us with the most remarkable spectacles in the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Colours of Nature
programme.desc: From subtle to shocking, colour enhances the beauty of the natural world. Colour is a language to most living things, whether as a warning, camouflage or a come on. But there's more to colour than just pigment. Colours define how many animals behave, how they mate and how they inter-act with one another. For us the beautiful and varied shades of the animal kingdom form a spec-tacular kaleidoscope of colours. With all the diversity in the world, sit back and enjoy all the col-ours of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Seasons Of The Otter
programme.desc: Most of the life of an otter goes unseen following a young otter family we'll reveal the secrets of the otter's world.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Kangaroos: Gabi Friebe is an experienced kangaroo nanny at the Long Grass Nature Refuge in Australia. Gabi is worried: Some of her fosterlings are not doing well at all, and the little kangaroo Skippy is spending his first day alone outside. But why is he taking so long to come home at night? Mice, Duikers & Co.: Students Laura and Susanne are spending an unusual vacation on the “Daktari Farm” in South Africa. Besides spending their days nursing mice babies and African bush squirrels, they take care of a blind antelope baby named Emily.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Wombat Brady: Manfred and Helen Heide run a wombat sanctuary they call the “Wombat Hilton”. Brady is their latest “guest”. Together with wombat girl Chloé, Brady spends his days being cuddled by the Heides and hanging around in a fabric pouch. Soon he gets bored, however, and is happy to go an excursion... Wolves: Pascale Jüch has an unusual job: As a surrogate mom to three little wolves, she spends her day cuddling and nursing the wolf babies. In order for her to teach them proper pack behaviour, it is important that they accept her as the alpha animal. Pascale hopes that soon she will be able to integrate her fosterlings into another wolf pack. Kudu: Kudu Baby Kuduku was raised on the farm “Friedensort” in Namibia. The farmer's son Marco Syvertsen is Kuduku's best friend. But now it is time for the little Kudu to return to the wild. Marco and Kuduku take their first walk together outside the farm. Will Kuduku eventually appreciate the intimidating wilderness?

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Pademelons: Pademelon baby Taila was raised by her nanny Julia Butler-Ross in her house. She is now growing too big and is to resettle in a new space outside. Obviously she is not to grow lonely there, and so Julia has a big surprise in store for Taila. Zebra: On the “Solitaire Guest Farm” in Namibia lives a young Zebra named Stripies who has just about reached puberty. His nanny Simone Swarts is amazed how similar his state is to human puberty: The zebra is moody and keeps pushing its borders. All the other animals are rather annoyed with Stripies, and Simone is worried that he will not be able to make friends with them again. But suddenly there is a small calf that does not budge from his sight. Has Stripies found a friend after all? Flying Foxes: Animal carer Roy Bäthe has found two motherless flying foxes at the zoo in Erfurt. He is now raising little Yoda and Ewok at home. They are developing great. There is just one question to be answered: How is Roy going to teach them how to fly?

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Penguins: Injured and orphaned penguins have found a new temporary home at the “Penguin Rehab Centre” in South Africa. Nanny Fran Grobbelaars newest foundling is a small penguin named Madala. In her care, Madala has recovered from the extreme injuries he had suffered – but now he has to learn many things anew. Fran teaches him everything he needs to know, from catching fish to swimming. Lion Baby Alvin: The small lion named Alvin was rescued from a Bavarian farm where he was not kept according to his kind. He was sick and shy. Through the efforts of the “Wildlife Nannies” crew, he found a new home at Joe Bodemann's park for film animals. It takes Joe some time to win the Alvin's trust – but eventually Alvin grows to be a healthy and strong lion. The training for his film career can start! Springbok: The Namibian “Omaruru Gamelodge” is a small paradise for more than 2.000 animals. Nanny Thomas Domenig is an expert of raising them all. We join him on the daily excitement of raising wild animals – his newest nurse child is a small springbok.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Gut Aiderbichl: Many stories take place at the animal sanctuary Gut Aiderbichl. Nanny Rico Fernando raises five orphaned fox babies as well as a tiny goatling named Franziska. Martina Neureiter makes sure an injured fawn named Bambi learns to walk again. Sharks: When they are fully grown, they look wild and scary – but even sharks do not enter this world as adults. Nanny Birger Kreutz knows how tiny and helpless predatory fish can be right after they are born. In this episode, he tries to get his newborn dogfish to join some stingray children in a new aquarium – not an easy task! Bat-eared Foxes: Margit Hülpert is the founder of the “bat-eared fox project” in Namibia that is dedicated to scientific research and preservation of the unusual animals. For the past months, she has been raising bat-eared fox named Monty. Now the time has come to release him to the wild. They spend a last fun, but emotional day together.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Seals: Tanja Rosenberg is part of a seal rescue team in Friedrichskoog in the far north of Germany. Many seals lose their mothers for various reasons each year – Tanja and her crew make sure they survive, grow strong and can be returned to the North Sea again. Birds of Prey: Karl Fischer is the manager of the sanctuary for birds of prey. One of his specialties is the upbringing of bald eagles – like his newest fosterling Ingo. But Karl does not want Ingo to grow up alone and so he introduces him to a young eagle owl named Klaus. Though the two make sure that Karl knows how much they dislike each other and keep bickering and biting one another, they are both suspiciously sad, when Klaus is moving to a different zoo… Platypus: Kangaroo-nanny Margit Cianelli is nervous: A tiny motherless platypus has been found by hikers and been brought to her house. She has never cared for a platypus and is not sure if everything is alright with the little guy. A visit at the vet's is to bring clarity.

programme.title: Wildlife Nannies
programme.desc: Kangaroo Kira: Karin Traub has dedicated her life to saving injured and orphaned kangaroo babies in Australia. Currently she shares her trailer with a whole kangaroo kindergarten! Her newest problem child is the tiny kangaroo Kira. Kira does not want to drink her milk. Luckily Karin has a big bag of tricks and knows just what to do. Racoon Dog Mitja: Nicola Gölzner is raising an unusual little animal at her house. But racoon dog Mitja is so cute, that even her dog has taken a liking to the little guy. Today the three of them go to check out a wildlife park near Nicola's house, where Mitja is to live as soon as he is big enough. How will Mitja like his future home? Warthogs: On the “Daktari Farm” in Namibia two warthog babies named Lilly and Piggy are utterly besotted with their nanny Bärbel Hahn. They have completely accepted her as a surrogate mom – but just like real children, they do not always behave the way she wants them to. As soon as she turns her back, the little rascals are up to new pranks. Lucky for them that Bärbel's patience is sheer inexhaustible.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: An orphaned baby baboon has enjoyed the love of its human foster mother at CARE, an emergency wildlife center. How will he respond when he meets his first baboon friend? This is an important milestone in becoming a member of a troop. Animal rescuers from the Wildlife Foundation in Namibia need to catch a young female cheetah in captivity this is tricky and potentially dangerous work. Their plan is to reunite her with her mother so that she can learn how to function and survive in the wild. Bush babies are especially cute so sometimes they are kept as pets. The care givers at Riverside Wildlife Centre put emotions aside and prepare one for release in the wild.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: Rhino horn is more valuable than gold and more recent increases in poaching leaves the survival of this species at risk. Every day a rhino monitoring team at Somkhanda Game Reserve keeps track of a rhino and her calf, but skilful tracking and telemetry don't guarantee that they'll find them. When it comes to their milk feed, the competition is fierce for four orphaned baby dassies at Daktari Wildlife Orphanage. Their care taker forms strong bonds with the animals he looks after and decides to visit the rocky outcrop where he's released a dassie – can he find his friend? A baby porcupine is hand-reared at Tenikwa Wildlife and Rehabilitation Centre. Can his surrogate mom teach him to forage otherwise they won't be able to release him back in the wild.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: Raising wildlife can be a long-term commitment: elephant calves are dependent on their mothers for three years and chimpanzees rely on special attention for about seven. In this episode, we visit the Lilayi Elephant Nursery in Zambia and the Mefou Primate Park in Cameroon to meet the highly committed people who take on this responsibility. Night or day, a baby elephant can never be left alone and many of the chimpanzee orphans arrive highly traumatised. Their care givers need to work very hard to restore their trust and will to live. At the Kwaggasvlaktes Wildlife Centre in South Africa some baby Wildebeest rely on heart-warming help from humans and an unusual sheep to survive.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: An African penguin chick has survived expulsion from the nest, but can surgery help an adult who's been savaged by a seal to see the ocean again? All in a day's job at the South African Marine Rehabilitation and Education Centre where a caregiver's fingers also narrowly escape the razor-sharp beak of a gannet. Something stands in the way of a high spirited young caracal, but the Sondela Wildlife Centre are hatching a plan to secure his release back in the wild. Two baby warthogs at the Gitta Martula Rehabilitation Wildlife Centre still prefer their apples to their potatoes but, slowly and surely, they are being adjusted to living in their natural environment – the whole wild world awaits.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: Rehabilitating and teaching young wild dog packs to hunt is what Margerita does at Erindi Private Game Reserve in Namibia. Margerita had discovered many of the wild dog packs in the region had succumb to illness and died. Margerita took it upon herself to raise an abandoned young pack of wild dogs. She teaches them to run as a pack, hunt as a pack and survive as a pack thus ensuring when the time comes the wild dog pack will survive in the wild. Alma's passion for yellow baboons in Malawi brought her to the African continent to live-out her passion. Her daily chores, activities and inspections keep her busy. Two yellow baboons meet each other for the first time when the gate separating them is opened. One of South Africa's smallest antelope known as a Cape Grys Bokkie loves playing hide ‘n seek it seems. When it comes to feeding the young antelope it always proves to be a challenge. However, this could be the nature of this timid little antelope.

programme.title: Raising Wildlife
programme.desc: Lindi, the one-eyed Lion cub lives at Wildthingz Lodge situated in the northern region of South Africa. Having one eye does not restrict Lindi from been a playful and energetic lion cub. Skabenga, a young Hyena, also lives at Wildthingz. Skabenga has almost become part of the family, and his bond to his caretaker cannot be denied. Wolves in Africa? Tsitsikamma Wolf Sanctuary is an orphanage to abandoned wolves. Raising wolves on the other hand is not always an easy job. This is the case with an energetic seven-month-old wolf, Raul who knows no boundaries. We meet Victor Hugo at Somkhanda where the motto is to keep all the animals wild while rehabilitating them. We will meet a herd of Zebra and their young and discover that the dynamics in the family group are not always what they seem. Victor also rescues a family of owl hatchlings and ensures their safety by building a new house in a nearby tree.

programme.title: Jack Hanna
programme.desc: Join Jungle Jack as he samples some cultural delicacies on his way to discovering amazing creatures!

programme.title: Echo And The Elephants Of Amboseli
programme.desc: In the relentless drought, the youngest calves are starving. Hunger is driving elephants to forage on surrounding farms. Echo's keeps her family to the swamp. Rains arrive, but after five days, the much-needed downpours dwindle to sparse showers.

programme.title: The Rhino Orphanage

programme.title: The Rhino Orphanage

programme.title: The Rhino Orphanage

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake travels to magical Holidayland with Honey Pie Pony, searching for the perfect presents to give her friends. It all ends well, though, when Strawberry's friends remind her that the best gift of all is the gift of friendship.

programme.desc: Adora hopes to get some much needed RandR in the most magical place in all of Etheria: Mystacor.

programme.title: She-Ra and the Princesses of Power
programme.desc: The princesses of Etheria gather for their royal conference.

programme.title: She-Ra and the Princesses of Power
programme.desc: Adora and a motley crew go on a mission to infiltrate the Fright Zone.

programme.title: She-Ra and the Princesses of Power
programme.desc: Devastated by the apparent death of Entrapta, the princesses disband from the Rebellion.

programme.title: She-Ra and the Princesses of Power
programme.desc: Adora and Catra are forced to work together to free themselves from holographic memory chambers.

programme.title: She-Ra and the Princesses of Power
programme.desc: Adora finally gets face time with Light Hope inside the Crystal Castle.

programme.title: She-Ra and the Princesses of Power
programme.desc: Adora, Bow and Glimmer return to Bright Moon just as the Horde begins its onslaught.

programme.title: She-Ra and the Princesses of Power
programme.desc: Catra stretches her muscles in her new role as number two to Hordak.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors use real stomach acid to do a gross experiment to reveal how your digestion process starts and Dr Chris proves that the younger you are the more sounds you can hear!

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors show that your breath is just as unique as your fingerprints and Dr. Chris goes behind the scenes of medical drama "Casualty" to see how they make fake body parts look so realistic.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: Dr. Chris and Dr. Xand show you an awesome body trick to amaze your friends with and reveal another way your brilliant body heals itself if you get injured.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: Dr. Chris uses a tiny camera to reveal what happens in his throat when he swallows food and Dr. Xand enters a special chamber that removes sensory data from his brain.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors reveal how much power there is in your farts, Dr Chris trains as a nurse on a ward full of robotic patients and in 'Ouch and About' Dr Xand opens up the brand new 'Ouch-Mobile'.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors reveal how far your heart can pump blood, Dr Xand meets a life-saving dog, and in 'Ouch and About', Dr Chris is in the 'Ouch-Mobile'.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors reveal what comes out of your mouth when you cough, Dr Xand gets a front row seat at a life-changing operation and there are more intriguing medical mysteries to solve in Ouch and About.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors reveal how you taste with your nose as well as your tongue, Dr Xand is on call with the emergency services, and Dr Chris discovers how 3D printing works when he prints his own skull.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors fill their bladders -quite literally- to show what happens when you go to the toilet. Dr Chris meets a robot that can zap bad cells and Dr. Xand is in the 'Ouch-Mobile'.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors try to trick their body temperatures, the 'Ouch-Mobile' is open for more intriguing medical mysteries, and Dr Xand tries to stop his body giving him away when he tells fibs!

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors make an unusual type of cheese with Xand's body bacteria, find out what to do if someone breaks their leg in First Aid, and Dr Chris meets a man with a bionic eye.

programme.title: Operation Ouch
programme.desc: The Doctors set fire to food [all in the name of science!] Dr Xand goes to an Asthma clinic, and in 'Ouch and About' the Ouch-Mobile opens its doors to solve more intriguing medical mysteries.

programme.title: Back To School
programme.desc: When the NEXO-Knights are sloppy in a fight with Jestro, Merlok 2.0 sends them back to the Knights' Academy. Not only for a brush-up but to find out what Jestro is after in its hallowed halls. As they Knights battle Jestro – in a somewhat haphazard and lazy way – they discuss the fact that Jestro never wins and that they're always getting over on him. Despite defeating the jerky Jester and his evil Book, Merlok 2.0 discovers that something about the attack on the village is tied to the Knights' Academy. It looks like that's where they'll strike next. It wouldn't hurt for the Knights to go “back to school” to watch for Jestro and for some refresher training. Merlok is sure Jestro wants something at the Academy, but he doesn't know what it is. The Knights are very much “fish out of water” at the academy, even with the “cool kids” – Robin and Ava – showing them around. The next generation of Knights is being taught to integrate the NEXO-Magic of Merlok into their fighting techniques and these kids are good (so good, they initially kick the lazy, know-it-all Knight's butts!) Their old principal, the ever-ornery Headmaster Brickland, sets them straight: they're all still slackers, and they better get their acts together (especially Lance!) The Knights take shifts as “library helpers” much to the delight of the old squirebot librarian, MARGE. They're convinced Jestro is after a book…isn't he always after a book? When Jestro attacks, the Knights help organize the students and there's a big fight in the Academy Library versus Jestro and his Monsters. They drive Jestro off without letting him steal any books! Victory! But as Jestro leaves, we see him holding what the Book of Monsters really wanted: a snow globe with a castle in it that was sitting on the librarian's desk. In the Lava Lands, Jestro throws the snow globe into a volcano and it frees the massive Castle of Monstrux from its snowy magic prison

programme.title: Greed Is Good?
programme.desc: When Jestro uses the Book of Greed to rob Auremville of its riches, he also gets Lance's parents as prisoners and these demanding rich folks are too much for even monsters to handle. Jestro's not sure what to do with his new castle in the Lava Lands. “Every castle needs a big chamber filled with treasure” says the Book of Monsters so they've got to go out looting! The BOM convinces Jestro to call forth GENERAL MAGMAR, and Jestro appoints him as “general manager” of “castle operations,” much to Magmar's dismay. Meanwhile, Jestro and the BOM find the BOOK OF GREED. This will allow them to make greedy, rapacious monsters that will bring them all the treasure they want to fill their castle! They'll head to the richest town in the realm – AUREMVILLE! Meanwhile, in Auremville, the NEXO-Knights arrive for a huge “Gold is Good” ball at Lance's house. When the Monsters finally arrive in Auremville, a battle breaks out and the greedy monsters make off with everything…including Lance's parents, Goldie and Cuthbert Richmond who are clad in the finest golden garments and so look valuable! Lance will now be forced to live…poor. The Knights have to get his parents back. Meanwhile, his parents – Cuthbert and Goldie Richmond –think their imprisonment in Jestro's gold-filled castle is a “spa holiday” with the worst service. They order Magmar and the monsters around like servants and drive them all crazy. The Knights pile into the Fortrex and head for the Lava Lands, formulating a plan along the way to free Lance's parents. In a village half-way to Jestro's Castle, the Knights run into…Jestro and his monsters headed the other way with Lance's parents and all the gold they've stolen. There's a big fight, until they realize they might have the same goal and decide to deal. The BOM wants to give them back all the gold and Lance's parents. Lance says “No deal.” “We'll take the gold, you can keep my parents.” It's a weird and comical stand-off. Finally, Clay convinces Lance he has to take his parents back even if they don't always get along (and, it's the only way to get the gold back.)

programme.title: Peak Performance
programme.desc: The search for the Sandman leads Mateo and the others to the tallest mountain in the Dream World, but Lunia's hourglass puts a target on their backs.

programme.title: The Anomaly
programme.desc: A surprise visit from the Chief Inspector of the Night Bureau forces Mateo to hide Z-Blob, who goes on an adventure in the heart of Brooklyn.

programme.title: The Digiverse And Beyond, The Elemental Masters
programme.desc: Tackling a puzzle about technology in Ninjago. A riddle requires the Ninja to consider the powers and history of the Elemental Masters.

programme.title: The Jade Princess
programme.desc: In the wake of an assassination attempt targeting the royal family, the ninja invite the royal princess, HARUMI, into their makeshift family. Lloyd glimpses someone escaping over the rooftops with a sack on their back. Fearing it is the kidnapped princess, Lloyd takes up the chase! He discovers to his surprise, however, that the masked intruder is none other than the princess herself, who is not quite as docile or meek as she pretends. She has a habit of sneaking out of her chambers at night to feed the homeless who surround the Imperial Palace. This revelation only endears her to Lloyd more and Harumi and Lloyd quickly strike up a friendship. Unfortunately, trouble catches up to the ninja when the Sons Of Garmadon target the second Oni mask and attack the palace. The scope of the attack proves more than the Ninja were prepared for as the Sons of Garmadon detonate a series of subterranean bombs, bringing down part of the palace. Lloyd and Harumi escape onto the flying ship, DESTINY'S BOUNTY. But the victory is a pyrrhic one. The second Oni Mask has been stolen and Harumi's parents and Master Hutchins are feared lost.

programme.title: The Pranks
programme.desc: Stephanie finds the perfect front page story for the school paper! It's almost too good to be true. Emma and Andrea go undercover to solve the mystery of the two Jacobs.The friends are using their talents to pull a prank on Olivia. But who gets the last laugh?

programme.title: Andrea's Big Moment
programme.desc: When famous pop star Livi comes to Heartlake City to judge a talent show, our five friends Olivia, Mia, Andrea, Emma and Stephanie all decide to enter – and one of them, in particular, really wants to win but faces some serious challenge from an old rival. A talent show has come to Heartlake City and all the girls enter the talent competition hoping to impress the judges, especially their idol, pop star Chloe. No one wants a shot at stardom more than Andrea, which is why the other girls are surprised when she doesn't show up to practice before the first round of the competition. Very nearly eliminated prior to the big show, Andrea panics and throws herself into an intense practice-training mode, even shutting her friends out when they're trying to help her with her performance. Just moments before her live TV debut, Andrea realizes she's missing a key element to her act. Can she pull it off without her friends? And are the other girls willing to help after being pushed away?

programme.title: Kate's Island
programme.desc: The five friends leave for science camp that takes place on an island and during the trip, they meet the stand-offish Kate – but they soon realize that there's more to her than meets the eyes and that you shouldn't judge the book by its cover. No one is more excited than Olivia about science camp at a beautiful rocky offshore island with a lighthouse. But Olivia quickly gets off on the wrong foot with a mysterious girl named Kate who seems obsessed with a medallion necklace and the island's rumored history of pirates and treasure. Even worse for Olivia, Kate seems to be growing extra chummy with Mia, even sneaking around the island with her late at night. With the help of Stephanie, Emma and Andrea, Olivia is determined to get to the bottom of the girls' secret friendship and find out once and for all what they're searching for on the island, but they better not let Mrs. Stevens catch them out of their cabin past curfew.

programme.title: The Grand Hotel
programme.desc: The five friends help out at The Grand Hotel that Andrea's family just bought during what was supposed to be a quiet weekend until a hotel critic arrives unexpectedly. The girls are ready to do everything to make a great impression – but things don't quite go according to plan. Andrea, Stephanie, Olivia, Emma and Mia have come for breakfast at the fanciest hotel in town, now owned by Andrea's family. When Andrea's aunt has to leave town, the girls volunteer to run the hotel for the weekend with the help of quirky Grandma Jean. This is the perfect job for Stephanie's expert managerial skills but then the hotel suddenly fills to capacity with guests, including a hotel critic that can make or break the hotel's reputation! Now the girls find themselves in over their heads juggling reception, childcare, food prep and waitressing, a dog-walking service and a testy chef who quits on them in the middle of dinner. Unfortunately, much of this is their own fault. Can the girls save the Grand Hotel from themselves?

programme.title: Ninjaball Run
programme.desc: When the ninja learn that Darreth's Mojo Dojo is to be destroyed and Darreth doesn't have the money to save it, our heroes enter the annual NinjaBall Run to win its cash prize in order to save the dojo. As Lloyd continues to train and unlock his hidden powers, the Ninja learn land developers are going to destroy Darreth's Mojo Dojo unless Darreth can find the money to save it. The Ninja decide to enter their Ninja Tank into the annual NinjaBall Run cross-country rally to try to win its cash prize. Revisiting past characters who also enter the race, the high-speed derby will only grow more dangerous as Garmadon enters his Black Bounty, realizing this is the perfect chance to use his Mega-Weapon and rid the ninja. In the end, the Ninja will discover a few secrets within their Ninja Tank, allowing them to beat Garmadon's Bounty by a millisecond and saving Darreth's Dojo with their well-deserved cash prize. And as a final surprise, the Ninja steal back the Destiny's Bounty and make it their own once again.

programme.title: Child's Play
programme.desc: When the Ninja interrupt Lord Garmadon from using his Mega-Weapon to ‘turn back the clock' and bring to life the fossils of a monster, the Ninja are accidentally hit and turned into kids and are now forced to enlist Lloyd's help to battle the awakened monster. Tired of intense training, Lloyd yearns to play and enjoy his youth. The Ninja remind Lloyd that along with great power, comes great responsibility. When the Ninja interrupt Lord Garmadon from using his Mega-Weapon to ‘turn back the clock' and bring to life the fossils of a monster, the Ninja are accidentally hit and soon discover they've been turned into kids! Seeking Lloyd's help and relying on childhood antics, the five ninja kids must destroy the reawakened monster that's hunting them in the city. In the end, Lloyd makes the difficult decision and sacrifices his youth in order to save them from the beast, turning forward the clock and turning Lloyd into a young man. Sensei Wu fears now that since Lloyd is young man, the final battle of good versus evil approaches sooner than he had ever expected.

programme.title: The Surge
programme.desc: After a time of peace, the Ninja are called back into action. The spirit of the defeated Overlord has infected the electric grid of Ninjago and is using all available technology to exact his revenge on Lloyd. Nowhere is safe but fortunately, the Ninja have been given the powerful Techno Blades. But the only problem is that they have no idea how to use them. Time has passed since the Ultimate Battle, and while the Golden Ninja continues to watch over Ninjago, the other Ninja have become complacent as schoolteachers. When a mysterious invitation from genius Cyrus Borg brings them to the futuristic New Ninjago City, trouble surges when a digital virus of the Overlord takes over technology. The Golden Ninja makes a heroic entrance, only to find out his Golden Power supercharges the enemy. Sensei Wu stays behind so that the Ninja can escape with the only thing that can save Ninjago's future – Borg's Techno Weapons.

programme.title: The Art Of The Silent Fist
programme.desc: The Ninja go into hiding but an army of the Digtial Overlord's evil robotic Nindroids are hunting them wherever they go. Their only chance is to learn the secrets of the Techno blades and use them to cut all power to save Ninjao from this technological menace. The Ninja hide out at a safe place void of technology – Sensei Garmadon's Sanctuary. That night, Zane is visited by Borg's female robo-assistant, Pixal. She warns them that the Digital Overlord is after Lloyd's Golden Power in order to become flesh, but the Techno Blades can reboot the system and erase him for good. After a highly advanced army of Nindroids attacks the Sanctuary, Lloyd flees with his father into the wilderness of Ninjago, while the rest set off on a quest to turn off all power in Ninjago, so that they can erase the Digital Overlord once and for all.

programme.title: The Book Of Total Badness
programme.desc: The NEXO-Knights must fight Jestro's Monsters of Destruction which are attacking all over the Kingdom. But Merlok smells a trap…is he right? In a dark, destroyed part of the kingdom, Jestro arrives at the ruins of a HUGE castle. The Book of Monsters knows this place: the great evil wizard Monstrux lived here. The Book sniffs out some powerful magic in the ruins: “The Book of Destruction.” With this, The Book tells Jestro they can destroy the NEXO-Knights and then attack the King's Castle and cause massive destruction. Jestro creates destructive monsters and sends them all over the realm, so the Knights must separate and fight single combats all over the place. The Knights lose these battles (lesson: they have to work as a team!) And, just when the Knights are scattered across the Kingdom, Jestro attacks the King's Castle! It looks like all is lost. When Aaron, who has escaped, returns to see Jestro's attack, he realizes he must gather his fellow Knights. Merlok says it's very dangerous, but Aaron says he must take the risk to gather the Knights and save Knighton.

programme.title: The Might And The Magic
programme.desc: Aaron races across the kingdom to gather the NEXO-Knights as Jestro and his Destructive Monsters attack the King's Castle. The Knights must get back to the capitol in time, or Jestro will rule the kingdom! Jestro, while still attacking the King's Castle, has second thoughts until the Book of Monsters orders the mad jester to feed him another magic book he's found (and has been saving.) It's the powerful “Book of Revenge!” Now, combining the powers of Revenge and Destruction, Jestro and his monsters will get their vengeance on King Halbert and the Kingdom of Knighton. Jestro and his monsters lay siege…will they destroy the castle?! Meanwhile, Aaron races across the Kingdom gathering the NEXO-Knights one at a time. As each Knight rejoins the group, they become a more and more powerful team. They even get Robin to join them as the Black Knight in his repaired power suit. Our Knights arrive, finally regrouped, fight off Jestro, save the castle and the King. It's a glorious victory! The King is so pleased after seeing how the brave NEXO-Knights saved his kingdom, he allows Macy to join them as an official NEXO-Knight.

programme.title: Back To School
programme.desc: When the NEXO-Knights are sloppy in a fight with Jestro, Merlok 2.0 sends them back to the Knights' Academy. Not only for a brush-up but to find out what Jestro is after in its hallowed halls. As they Knights battle Jestro – in a somewhat haphazard and lazy way – they discuss the fact that Jestro never wins and that they're always getting over on him. Despite defeating the jerky Jester and his evil Book, Merlok 2.0 discovers that something about the attack on the village is tied to the Knights' Academy. It looks like that's where they'll strike next. It wouldn't hurt for the Knights to go “back to school” to watch for Jestro and for some refresher training. Merlok is sure Jestro wants something at the Academy, but he doesn't know what it is. The Knights are very much “fish out of water” at the academy, even with the “cool kids” – Robin and Ava – showing them around. The next generation of Knights is being taught to integrate the NEXO-Magic of Merlok into their fighting techniques and these kids are good (so good, they initially kick the lazy, know-it-all Knight's butts!) Their old principal, the ever-ornery Headmaster Brickland, sets them straight: they're all still slackers, and they better get their acts together (especially Lance!) The Knights take shifts as “library helpers” much to the delight of the old squirebot librarian, MARGE. They're convinced Jestro is after a book…isn't he always after a book? When Jestro attacks, the Knights help organize the students and there's a big fight in the Academy Library versus Jestro and his Monsters. They drive Jestro off without letting him steal any books! Victory! But as Jestro leaves, we see him holding what the Book of Monsters really wanted: a snow globe with a castle in it that was sitting on the librarian's desk. In the Lava Lands, Jestro throws the snow globe into a volcano and it frees the massive Castle of Monstrux from its snowy magic prison

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Sari has to defend the Autobot home base from invading Constructicons.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Megatron comes up with a plan to build a space bridge to Cybertron.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Optimus Prime prepares to return to Earth to face the mysterious Great Evil he's prophesied to Bumblebee.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Optimus Prime, Bee and his team must use everything they've learned together to stop Megatronus.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The Autobots split into two teams.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: In a last-ditch effort, the Autobots and Decepticons work together to try and save their universe.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Starscreams fate is revealed a judge of the Quintessons and the interrogation of the Cybertronians begins.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Windblade battles her way into Iaconus.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Windblade wakes Iaconus only to discover the Quintessons have summoned their own titan.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Laconus falls and all looks lost until Megatron returns from the Multiverse with a new plan.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: In an attempt to find his missing friend, Bumblebee goes on a spy mission into Decepticon territory.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The only way to defeat Unicron is to wake the minicons inside him and attack from within as well as without.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The purpose and meaning behind the endless war is revealed.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: With Unicron seemingly defeated, Galvatron insists on settling things with Optimus Prime.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The second of the relic hunts takes Optimus Prime to the Antarctic.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The disabling of Soundwave's deployer creates a unique opportunity for Ratchet and Wheeljack.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The final relic in the hunt may prove more deadly to Bulkhead than the Insecticon warriors who pursue him.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Pussywillow Pixies: Clumsy Smurf's misadventures take him across a river into Pussywillow Hollow. There he meets the Pixies – charming and shapely beings whose enemies, the Wartmongers, will meet the Smurfs in some unforeseen ways.

programme.title: The Whole Smurf And Nothing But The Smurf
programme.desc: The evil wizard Gargamel is able to capture the Smurfette. Now he is going to give her a truth serum and know all of Papa Smurf' secrets… But then Gargamel makes a big mistake and gives the Smurfette a fib formula!

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Unsound Smurf: In an effort to give Papa some quiet time, Brainy uses one of Papa's spells to create a magical sound barrier around the village. Papa cannot reverse the spell and sends the Smurflings, who are trapped outside the Village, to a noisemaker. Baby takes a bottle of sound from the noisemaker, which breaks the barrier.

programme.title: Spelunking Smurfs
programme.desc: The starving Smurfs discover a cave that has a stash of frozen food inside guarded by an equally frozen troll who was imprisoned because of his greed by a magic diamond. As the Smurfs defrost the cave, Gargamel steals the food, but the magic diamond transforms him into an icy mass and the freed troll happily helps the Smurfs carry the food back to their village.

programme.title: Poet's Writers' Block
programme.desc: The Smurfs want a poem written for each of them. The pressure overwhelms Poet, so he runs away seeking inspiration. The Wilybeast King captures Poet and forces him to write a poem to cheer up the King's daughter. Poet learns that motivation must come from the heart and cannot be forced.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Chief Record Smurf: Due to a wild experiment Clumsy is able to store endless knowledge into his memory. Gargamel captures him, trying to unlock Papa Smurf's invisibility formula, but at the last minute, Clumsy tricks him with the wrong ingredients.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Greedy Goes On Strike: Get up early in the morning, before any other Smurf is out of bed, to prepare a scrumptious Smurfberry muffin. Spend all morning on preparing Smurf delicacies for lunch. Than all afternoon to have dinner ready – and delicious – on time. And do you get so much as a thank you form the Smurfs? So Greedy Smurf quits. The Village is in trouble... And soon, so is Greedy.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - A Clockwork Smurf: Handy has built a very complex Smurf machine that does everything. It proves dangerous when it nearly destroys the village, but comes in useful when, with the help of the other Smurfs, it saves the young prince Gerard's kingdom from the queen aunt Imperia's dastardly intentions.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Last Smurfberry: Papa Smurf organizes a crew to sail to Smurfberry Island because Gargamel has killed all of the trees except one to trap the Smurfs. Gargamel follows them, but once more fails to catch them because the Witch of Smurfberry Island sends her nose-eating seagulls after them.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - It's A Puppy's Life: The evil wizard Gargamel has got hold of magical ectoplasmic crystal eggs that will enable him to change bodies with Papa Smurf. What better plan could there be? Disguised as Papa Smurf, he's going to lead all the Smurfs to his cauldron! But as fate would have it, Gargamel is changed into Puppy, the Smurf doggie...

programme.title: Reckless Smurfs
programme.desc: This time, Gargamel's plan cannot fail. Undeterred by Azrael's derisive cackling, the evil wizard perfects a magic substance called Daredevil Dust, which is going to change all the Smurfs into rash, incautious swashbucklers. Will Papa Smurf fall into Gargamel's trap? Will their enemy catch all the Smurfs? Will this story have a happy ending?

programme.title: Heavenly Smurfs
programme.desc: Once again, Gargamel's infallible plan to catch the Smurfs has been foiled. But the Smurfs play a trick on him: disguised as tiny blue angels, they will teach him to mend his ways...

programme.title: Have You Smurfed Your Pet Today?
programme.desc: The Smurfs get so involved with Handy's new toy that they forget to feed Puppy. Gargamel lures Puppy into a cage with food and holds him for ransom – the Smurfs' magic secrets. The Smurflings distract Gargamel with the toy long enough to rescue Puppy and they vow to never neglect him again.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Handy's Sweetheart: Handy learns it's better to have smurfed than not to have smurfed at all when he learns his true love is really a mermaid. Can they really live with each other forever?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Prince And The Hopper: What is that strange talking frog that Morlock the Wizard sends all of Prince Theodore's soldiers to catch? Is it a demon frog, as he says, or something else? Truth will out when Smurfette and the Smurflings meet the frog. They help to change this frog back into Prince Theodore himself, for he's been the victim of a spell cast by Morlock.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Smurf On Wood: The evil wizard Gargamel once again meets Esmeralda, the gypsy fortune-teller, and she finds a novel way of getting the gold coin that Gargamel imprudently shows her. But the Smurfs in their turn are tricked into believing there is such a thing as a good-luck tree...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Forget-Me-Smurfs: Little Sickly turns into a hero when he forgets his ailments and saves the rest of the Smurfs from the deadly forget-me flowers.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Jokey's Shadow: Accidents do happen. This time it is Jokey Smurf's mistake that ends up with his shadow coming to independent life! And the shadow loves to play practical jokes on the whole population of the Village of the Smurfs...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Lazy's Slumber Party: Poor Lazy! All he wants is a good night's rest. But no, all the Smurfs throw a party for him and, what's more, he gets dragged into a silly argument about painting giant Bigmouth's toes a bright tangerine red... It's a long, sleepless night for poor Lazy Smurf!

programme.title: The Rabbids in Santa's country.
programme.desc: When Gina & John get out of power in their polar station, they create a new way to produce electricity.

programme.title: The Rabbids rebel.
programme.desc: Thanks to the Teleporta-TV, the rabbids lay hands on a voodoo doll with strange powers…

programme.title: The Rabbids aim for the moon!
programme.desc: The only way for rabbids to get into their rocket to reach the moon is to go on a drastic diet!

programme.title: Rabbid Obsession.
programme.desc: The rabbids have decided to reach the moon... thanks to a big ladder.

programme.title: Rabbids at your service!
programme.desc: A rabbid gets ejected from his rocket as it crashes, and takes the place of a guide dog.

programme.title: Smile, Rabbids!
programme.desc: When a family tries to take a nice picture at a wedding ceremony, the Rabbids ruin everything.

programme.title: The Rabbids are back!
programme.desc: Things don't go according to plan when the Rabbids start following a hitchhiker.

programme.title: Rabbids robbery.
programme.desc: The Rabbids follow a burglary.

programme.desc: After discovering the joys of tennis, the Rabbids move the game onto an airport runway.

programme.title: Rabbids VS Technology.

programme.title: Smile, Rabbids!

programme.title: The Rabbids are having fun!
programme.desc: The Rabbids find a cellphone on the sidewalk. They're fascinated by the ring tone.

programme.title: When I Was Your Age
programme.desc: Paul, 10, is a typical little boy. Except that as soon as someone tells him When I was your age, he is immediately hurled back to the time when the person he is talking to was 10 too.

programme.title: When I Was Your Age
programme.desc: Paul, 10, is a typical little boy. Except that as soon as someone tells him When I was your age, he is immediately hurled back to the time when the person he is talking to was 10 too.

programme.title: The Amazing Spiez
programme.desc: Megan is teased by her brothers because she is a poor skateboarder. Then they are called for a mission to investigate strange occurrences in Dubai, involving buildings and other structures breaking down. Later, Megan goes missing after getting her new skateboard that she will use to show her brothers that she can skate. Lee, Marc and Tony find her with an army of skateboarders responsible for breaking down the buildings so they can create skateparks for themselves.

programme.title: The Amazing Spiez
programme.desc: The Spiez are sent to protect a famous TV spy actor from various incidents. To keep an eye on him, the Spiez are invited to be in his Spy show, with the guidance of their parents who think they won a contest. The actor is kidnapped and the Spiez rescue him.

programme.title: Get Blake
programme.desc: Blake must master playing a wooden fish for a battle-of-the-bands performance with Skye. Followed by Sixty Second Dennis.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis and his pet dog, Gnasher, live by just one rule: Kids should make all the rules!

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: The adventures of Dennis, his dog Gnasher, and their best friends JJ, Rubi and Pieface. The team cause havoc to Walter, Beanotown and Bash Street School, where all the rules go out the window.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher: Stunt Gran
programme.desc: Walter shows off some new tricks on his fancy skateboard, prompting Dennis to make a jibe that even his Gran could do tricks like that with such a high-tech boar and the bet is on! However, Walter secretly sabotages the trick, causing Gran to hang up her menacing shoes as a matter of public safety. It's up to Dennis, Gnasher and Angel Face to expose the truth and get Gran back to her wily self. Followed by more Dennis episodes.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis and his pet dog, Gnasher, live by just one rule: Kids should make all the rules!

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis and his pet dog, Gnasher, live by just one rule: Kids should make all the rules! Followed by Dance of the Seven Pies.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: To get his friends back from Walter's group, Dennis challenges Walter to a videogames battle. Followed by 'Fish Tale'.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: For a school project, Dennis and Walter both build robots. However, during an accident, both robots merge, causing Dennis and Walter to defend the school. Followed by 'No Joke.'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: With one backstage pass for a Ratbucket gig, Dennis and his friends pass themselves off as Ratbucket, only for the real Ratbucket not to be able to play at his own concert. Followed by 60 second Dennis and 'Bad Dad'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: After Dennis breaks his bike, Dad is unable to afford a new one, so Dennis convinces him to ask his boss for a raise. Unfortunately, Mr Scrimp reduces his wages instead. Dennis then trashes his house to convince Scrimp that his family are poor, so that Mr Scrimp will feel compelled to raise Dad's wages. Just as Scrimp has been taken in by Dennis' plan, Mum and Dad arrive home laden with shopping bags, resulting in Dad getting fired on the spot. Dennis resolves to get Dad his old job back by secretly causing chaos at his office. To restore order Scrimp rehires Dad, sealing the deal with a generous pay rise. Mum and Dad repair the house, but still don't have enough money to buy Dennis a new bike, instead it gets repaired with Dad's office rubber bands and paperclips. Followed by 60 Second Dennis and 'Dennis Hiccups'.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Believing Gnasher and Gnipper to be trapped in Beanotown Dog Pound, Dennis comes up with a plan to rescue his friends and bring them both home. Followed by more Dennis and Gnasher episodes.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: When Dennis breaks his skateboard again, Dad agrees to buy Dennis the best skateboard there is as a replacement if he is able to win a prize at the upcoming school award ceremony. Followed by 60 Second Dennis and 'Dance of the Seven Pies'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis tries to get a birthday present by saying thank you to his relatives. However, because of Dennis' naughty personality, they take his attempts in a negative way. Followed by 'Dennis Hiccups'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: To get his friends back from Walter's group, Dennis challenges Walter to a videogames battle. Followed by 60 Second Dennis and 'Masters of the House'.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: After a wrestling accident, Gnasher swallows a canister of laughing gas at the vet's. Dennis, along with Gnasher hide in the conference centre, where dad gives a speech. However, the canister leaks, causing Gnasher to fart laughing gas and having everyone laughing. Followed by 'Attack of the Walterbot'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis and Co. have to control Pie Face's obsession with pies during the Pie Festival. Otherwise, they will be grounded. Followed by 'Great Outdoors'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: On a school trip, Dennis finds out that he is descentant to a warrior tribe. He also discovers that Montanna Smith (a famous Indiana Jones-esque explorer and one of Walter's personal heroes) is actually a thief who's trying to steal the treasure of Dennis' ancestor. Will he be stopped before he gets his hands on the treasure? Followed by Dog Tired.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Mum has bought curtain material for the soon to be newly decorated kitchen, and asks Dad to look after it while she is out. However, Dad falls asleep and mistakenly agrees that Dennis can use it as a giant sail. When Dad realises that it has gone, he calls Dennis to bring it back, but unfortunately Dennis gets into a spot of bother with the colonel, who takes the curtain material off Dennis. In an attempt to steal it back, the garden being wrecked and the kitchen is left looking like a bombsite. Dennis, Gnasher and Dad have to clean up the whole kitchen and redecorate it in time for Mum returning. When Mum decides it is time to hang the new curtains, Dennis is about to get the curtain material, but Mum reveals that she has changed her mind and bought new curtains instead. Followed by 'Fangs for the Memories'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis, his friends and Walter's friends get marooned on a desert island when Dennis attempts to escape a boring field trip. Followed by Substitute Creecher

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis plays a prank on the citizens of Beanotown, causing them to evacuate, which causes trouble for Dennis later. Followed by 'No Place Like Home'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Gnasher and Gnipper enter a dog show, regardless of Walter saying they don't have true talent. He wants Athena and Miss Mini Wuff to win, but Walter has just underestimated Gnasher's true capabilities. Followed by Grounded.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Athena gets Gnasher envy when seeing a pampered version of Gnasher in a magazine and when she hears that Dennis owns an Abyssinian wire-haired tripe hound (Gnasher), she wants him. However, being an owner to Gnasher isn't all cracked up to be! Followed by 'Splash'

programme.desc: Robotnik announces that he will marry Queen Aleena in a plot to become the king of Mobius.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Robotnik launches a spy-orb that will uncover the Freedom Fighter's Knothole location, Sonic and Robot launch themselves up to the orbiting spy-sphere aboard a home-made rocket powered by Robotnik's discarded booster engine.

programme.sub-title: Blast to the Past Part II

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: In Part II, Sonic and Sally steal the Roboti-sizer plans, stop the Destroyer from polluting the Great Forest and save Little Sonic, Little Sally and Dulcy's mother, Sabina.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: While on a mission to the big city to replace Nicole's malfunctioning microchip, Sonic and Sally encounter the Guardian, a mysterious cloaked figure who guards a dormant wizard named Lazar and his computer of evil spells.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: During a mission to one of Robotnik's factories, Sally is taken prisoner by Robotnik and Snively, who construct a roboti-sized "Sally" clone, complete with Sally's personality, and mannerisms. The double tricks Sonic into rescuing "Sally".

programme.sub-title: Blast to the Past Part I

programme.desc: A group of Freedom Fighters is obliterated by a swarm of metallic fly-bots. When Manic goes looking for the insects, he is stung with serum.

programme.desc: Mr. Bean thinks he's just dug up a valuable coin in the back-garden.

programme.desc: Mr. Bean is trying to read a book but he is disturbed by the noise of Mrs. Wicket and her friend.

programme.desc: His landlady smells a fish when Mr Bean gets a bird.

programme.desc: Mr Bean takes to the air after being taken for a ride.

programme.desc: Mr. Bean fancies a game of golf.

programme.title: Save That Tree
